metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Explicitly specify in the plan (and AC) which call site applies `criterion_prompt_id` before `create_prompt`: either `_library_create` calls `create_prompt(criterion_prompt_id(id), text, ...)` directly (keeping the current call structure but using the sanitized id), OR `author_criterion_overlay` absorbs the `create_prompt` call so `_library_create` delegates both prompt-write and overlay-write to a single function — one approach must be canonical and stated.
findings.finding: The plan does not explicitly specify which call site is responsible for applying criterion_prompt_id when invoking create_prompt. Currently _library_create calls create_prompt(pid, ...) where pid is whatever the editor sends. After the change the editor sends the dotted criterion id (e.g. project.foo) as `id`. If _library_create still calls create_prompt(pid, ...) with the raw dotted id, that id contains a '.' and will fail the _valid_id check — reproducing the exact bug being fixed. The plan must explicitly state whether (a) _library_create calls create_prompt(criterion_prompt_id(id), text) directly, or (b) author_criterion_overlay absorbs the create_prompt call so _library_create only calls author_criterion_overlay(criterion_id, routing, rubric_text).
findings.location: Plan section 'The dotted criterion id flows through explicitly (remediation, run 2)' + editor.py _library_create (lines 724, 740)
findings.suggested_fix: The cleaner option (lower blast radius): keep create_prompt in _library_create but change the call to `create_prompt(criterion_prompt_id(criterion_id), text, repo_root=_repo_root)` where `criterion_id` is the dotted id from the form. Then `author_criterion_overlay` stays overlay-only and takes the dotted criterion_id. This is consistent with 'prompt-first, overlay-second' and requires only a one-line change to the create_prompt call site.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Correct the edit target for rubric-resolution: replace `criteria.model.build_descriptor` with `registry._descriptor_from_prompt`'s inner `_get_prompt` closure (registry.py:233-236) as the site where `criterion_prompt_id(criterion_id)` must be applied instead of the raw `f"{_PROMPT_ID_PREFIX}{criterion_id}"` construction.
findings.finding: `criteria.model.build_descriptor` is named as an edit target for routing rubric resolution via `criterion_prompt_id`, but it is a pure structural transformer that never constructs prompt ids — the actual id construction site is the `_get_prompt` closure inside `registry._descriptor_from_prompt` (registry.py:233-236), which calls `prompts.get_prompt(f"{_PROMPT_ID_PREFIX}{criterion_id}", repo_root=root)`. An implementer following the plan's edit-target list would modify `build_descriptor` and leave the real construction site unchanged.
findings.location: Scope section — 'descriptor resolution: criteria.model.build_descriptor / plan_review.registry resolve a project criterion's rubric via get_prompt(criterion_prompt_id(cid), repo_root=...)'
findings.suggested_fix: In `registry._descriptor_from_prompt`, change the inner closure from `prompts.get_prompt(f"{_PROMPT_ID_PREFIX}{criterion_id}", repo_root=root)` to `prompts.get_prompt(criterion_prompt_id(criterion_id), repo_root=root)`. `build_descriptor` itself needs no change — it already accepts a generic prompt_getter.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `src/rebar/llm/workflow/editor.py` to the Scope file list and specify how `_library_create`'s `author_criterion_overlay(str(_repo_root), pid, routing)` call at line 740 must be updated to pass the dotted criterion id under the new signature.
findings.finding: `editor.py::_library_create` is an unenumerated edit target: it calls `author_criterion_overlay(str(_repo_root), pid, routing)` at line 740, passing the raw user-supplied `pid` as the second positional argument. The plan's remediation section changes `author_criterion_overlay`'s signature to take the dotted `criterion_id` as its primary argument (not a prompt id), and the plan's AC (remediation) requires the editor form's id field carry the dotted id. The `editor.py` call site must also change (the dotted criterion id, not the prompt id, must be passed), but `editor.py` is absent from the Scope file list.
findings.location: Scope section (file list) / src/rebar/llm/workflow/editor.py:740

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/production_batch_runner.py` to the Scope file list and specify how lines 177-178 (prefix-stripping to recover the bare criterion id) must be updated to correctly recover `project.foo` from a `plan-review-project-foo` prompt-id using `criterion_prompt_id`'s inverse or an explicit map.
findings.finding: `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/production_batch_runner.py` is an unenumerated consumer that will silently mis-derive the criterion id for project criteria. Lines 177-178 strip `_PROMPT_ID_PREFIX` (`'plan-review-'`) from a `batch.criteria[i].prompt` value to normalize to a bare registry id. Under the plan, an authored project criterion's prompt-id is `plan-review-project-foo` (sanitized, hyphenated). Stripping `plan-review-` yields `project-foo` (dashed), not `project.foo` (dotted) — so `registry.by_id().get('project-foo')` returns `None`, the criterion is silently skipped, and the gate never runs the authored criterion.
findings.location: Scope section (file list) / src/rebar/llm/plan_review/production_batch_runner.py:177-178

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Correct the descriptor-resolution edit target: the prompt-id construction site is the `_get_prompt` closure in `registry.py::_descriptor_from_prompt` (lines 233-237), not `criteria.model.build_descriptor`; update the Scope/What section to name `_descriptor_from_prompt` explicitly.
findings.finding: The plan names `criteria.model.build_descriptor` as an edit target for routing rubric resolution ('descriptor resolution: `criteria.model.build_descriptor` / `plan_review.registry` resolve a project criterion's rubric via `get_prompt(criterion_prompt_id(cid), ...)`'), but `build_descriptor` is a pure transformer that never constructs prompt ids — it calls `prompt_getter(cid, root)` via an injected callable. The actual site that constructs the prompt id is the `_get_prompt` closure at `registry.py:233-237` (`prompts.get_prompt(f"{_PROMPT_ID_PREFIX}{criterion_id}", repo_root=root)`). Listing `build_descriptor` as the edit target misdirects the implementer.
findings.location: What — decouple section (descriptor resolution bullet) / src/rebar/llm/criteria/model.py:131-188 / src/rebar/llm/plan_review/registry.py:233-237
findings.suggested_fix: In `_descriptor_from_prompt`'s `_get_prompt` closure, replace `f"{_PROMPT_ID_PREFIX}{criterion_id}"` with `criterion_prompt_id(criterion_id)` (where `criterion_prompt_id` is the new canonical function). `build_descriptor` itself needs no change.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify that the `_get_prompt` closure inside `_descriptor_from_prompt` (registry.py lines 233–236) is updated to call `criterion_prompt_id(criterion_id)` before the `plan-review-` prefix concatenation, not after — document this as the concrete change site for the descriptor-resolution half of the mapping.
findings.finding: The `criterion_prompt_id` function is referenced as the single canonical id→prompt-filename derivation in both descriptor resolution (`_descriptor_from_prompt`) and editor authoring (`author_criterion_overlay`), but no decision rule or protocol is specified for how `_descriptor_from_prompt`'s existing `prompt_getter` lambda (`f"{_PROMPT_ID_PREFIX}{criterion_id}"`) is updated to use it — the plan says 'route BOTH sides through it' but does not co-locate the replacement rule with the `_get_prompt` closure that currently hard-codes the dotted form.
findings.location: plan section 'What — decouple the logical id from the physical filename'; src/rebar/llm/plan_review/registry.py lines 233-236 (`_get_prompt` closure in `_descriptor_from_prompt`)
findings.suggested_fix: Replace the `_get_prompt` closure body with `return prompts.get_prompt(criterion_prompt_id(criterion_id), repo_root=root)` where `criterion_prompt_id` computes `f"plan-review-{name.replace('.', '-', 1)}"` for a project id (or keeps the builtin form unchanged).

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify explicitly in the plan that `/library/create` POST body adds a `criterion_id` field (or reuses `id` with value `project.<name>`) distinct from the prompt-library id, AND that `_library_create` passes this field (not `pid`) to `author_criterion_overlay`'s new `criterion_id` parameter.
findings.finding: The plan's 'remediation run 2' section mandates that `author_criterion_overlay` takes the DOTTED `criterion_id` as its primary argument (not the prompt id), but the current function signature is `author_criterion_overlay(repo_root, prompt_id, routing)` and it recovers `cid` by stripping `plan-review-`. The plan does not specify a decision rule for how `_library_create` in `editor.py` constructs the dotted criterion id to pass — currently it passes `pid` (the prompt-library id typed in the form), not a dotted id.
findings.location: plan section 'The dotted criterion id flows through explicitly'; src/rebar/llm/workflow/criterion_preview.py line 521–535 (`author_criterion_overlay`); src/rebar/llm/workflow/editor.py line 740 (`author_criterion_overlay(str(_repo_root), pid, routing)`)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a plan specification for the form's id-field protocol: either (a) the user types only the `<name>` part and the form derives + displays the full `project.<name>` criterion id as a read-only preview, sending `criterion_id: 'project.<name>'` and `id: 'plan-review-project-<name>'` separately to `/library/create`; or (b) the form accepts `project.<name>` directly and the client-side validation regex is updated to allow exactly one dot preceded by `project`.
findings.finding: The editor authoring form's `AuthoringIdEntry` (rebarProvider.jsx) has label 'new id (letters/digits/dashes)' and accepts a free-text value that is sent verbatim as the `/library/create` `id` field. The plan's AC5 requires the form surfaces the resulting criterion id (`project.<name>`), but neither the plan nor the existing code specifies the protocol: does the user type the full `project.foo` (violating the form's own label), or type only `foo` and the form prepends `project.`? Without this decision rule the form either dead-ends (user types `project.foo` → backend `_valid_id` rejects the dot) or is opaque (user types `foo` but the criterion id is silently `project.foo`).
findings.location: plan AC5 ('The editor authoring form surfaces the resulting criterion id'); rebarProvider.jsx `AuthoringIdEntry` ~line 1089–1106; plan section 'The dotted criterion id flows through explicitly'
findings.suggested_fix: Option (a): add a read-only display field below `AuthoringIdEntry` showing `project.${a.id}` when `a.kind === 'criterion'`, send `criterion_id: 'project.' + newId` in the POST body, and keep `id: 'plan-review-project-' + newId` as the prompt-library id.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an ADR entry (or update CLAUDE.md / a design doc) capturing the `criterion_prompt_id` mapping pattern — the rationale (dotted ids are invalid filenames; logical id must be decoupled from physical storage), the one-way nature of the map, and the dot-free charset constraint — so future contributors understand why the indirection exists.
findings.finding: The plan introduces a novel architectural pattern — decoupling a logical criterion id from its physical filename via a canonical mapping function (`criterion_prompt_id`), explicitly analogizing to Semgrep/npm/Python conventions — but specifies no ADR update, CLAUDE.md entry, or design-doc artifact to capture this decision. Future contributors modifying `create_prompt`, `enumerate_library`, or the overlay loader may re-couple the id to the filename without understanding the intentional separation.
findings.location: Scope section / 'Files' list; no mention of docs/ADR update
findings.suggested_fix: Create `.rebar/docs/adr/NNNN-criterion-id-prompt-filename-decoupling.md` (or append to the existing ADR 0015 / ef7e entry) documenting: (1) why dotted ids can't be raw filenames, (2) the `criterion_prompt_id` function as the sole derivation, (3) the one-way / non-reversible nature of the map, and (4) the dot-free charset constraint for project names.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `tests/unit/test_criterion_preview.py`, `tests/unit/test_criteria_overlay.py`, and `CLAUDE.md` to the Scope file list with explicit notes on which fixtures must be renamed (dotted → hyphenated) and which documentation lines must be updated.
findings.finding: Test fixture files using dotted project-criterion prompt ids (e.g. `plan-review-project.no_print`, `plan-review-project.bar`, `plan-review-project.foo`) in `tests/unit/test_criterion_preview.py` and `tests/unit/test_criteria_overlay.py` are acknowledged in the plan as requiring rewriting, but are not enumerated in the Scope file list. Similarly, `CLAUDE.md` line 268 documents the old dotted filename convention (`.rebar/prompts/plan-review-project.<name>.md`) and will be factually incorrect after the change, but is absent from Scope.
findings.location: Scope section / tests/unit/test_criterion_preview.py:98,117,260,275,284-285,308 / tests/unit/test_criteria_overlay.py:78,97,168,206,239,301 / CLAUDE.md:268

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Update ADR 0015 (or add an ADR addendum) to document `criterion_prompt_id()` as the canonical id→prompt-filename mapping and the dot-free project-name charset rule, so the contract is discoverable by future contributors and operators.
findings.finding: The plan introduces a new architectural contract — `criterion_prompt_id()` as the single canonical id→prompt-filename mapping, plus the dot-free project-name charset constraint — but does not mention updating ADR 0015 (which governs the overlay namespace) or producing any other doc/ADR for these new rules. Operators and future contributors have no single reference for the constraint.
findings.location: Scope section and AC lines — 'criterion_prompt_id(criterion_id)'; 'Project names are constrained to a dot-free charset'; ADR 0015 referenced in Why section as 'ef7e/ADR 0015'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a concrete proving command for each AC — at minimum: (a) a named pytest invocation (file + test function) for `criterion_prompt_id` unit tests, charset validation, and the author→activate→load→run round-trip; (b) an explicit statement that the round-trip test calls the real `registry.resolve_descriptor` / `load_criteria` path (not a mock) so the cutover is exercised end-to-end.
findings.finding: No concrete proving command (pytest invocation, curl call, CLI command) is specified for any of the six acceptance criteria — every AC says what to check but not how to produce evidence of success.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria (both sets)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Merge AC1 and AC4 into a single AC for `criterion_prompt_id`'s full contract (mapping, totality, injectivity, no-fallback, located error), or clearly partition them so each AC covers non-overlapping verifiable properties.
findings.finding: AC1 and AC4 are near-duplicates: both specify properties of `criterion_prompt_id` (the mapping direction, built-in unchanged, project mapped to sanitized form). AC4 adds 'injective', 'no dotted fallback', and 'located error' — but these are also stated in the body text covered by AC1's 'ONLY id→prompt-filename derivation'. An implementer must read both to know what to verify, and a test author is unclear which AC a test for injectivity belongs under.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria: AC1 and AC4

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC (or extend AC2/AC4) requiring that old dotted fixture filenames (`plan-review-project.*.md`) are removed and replaced with sanitized equivalents, so the 'one clean path' guarantee is verifiable.
findings.finding: The Scope section explicitly describes rewriting test fixtures (`plan-review-project.no_print.md` → `plan-review-project-no-print.md`) as concrete implementation work, but no AC covers the removal of the old dotted fixture filenames.
findings.location: Scope section: 'the ef7e/overlay TEST FIXTURES that hand-place a dotted `plan-review-project.no_print.md` are just rewritten to the sanitized `project.no-print` → `plan-review-project-no-print.md`'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add explicit test cases for invalid project names (dotted name, underscore, digit-start, empty) asserting a located error is returned from `_validate_routing_entry` / `author_criterion_overlay`, so the new charset constraint is tested for failure paths, not just the happy-path round trip.
findings.finding: The testing plan covers only the happy-path round trip (author→activate→load→run); it does not address failure/invalid-input paths for the new user-facing authoring flow — specifically: a project name containing a dot (`project.foo.bar`), an underscore (`project.foo_bar`), a name starting with a non-alnum character, or an empty name. These are all now 'located errors' introduced by the new charset constraint, but no AC or test description covers them.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC3 ('a name with a `.` is a located error') and AC4 ('a dotted/underscored project name is a located error')
findings.suggested_fix: Add an AC such as: 'Invalid project names (containing `.` beyond the namespace dot, `_`, starting with a non-alnum char, or empty) are rejected with a located error at overlay load or authoring time; unit tests assert each case returns the expected error.'

findings.finding: Acceptance-criteria / definition-of-done quality issues.
findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.finding: No file_impact declared on a leaf work ticket.
findings.suggested_fix: Record the {path, reason} files this work will touch (e.g. via set_file_impact) so the attestation is scoped to them.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add at minimum a documented API-level E2E check for the editor flow: a curl/httpx call that posts `{id: 'project.foo', routing: {...}}` to `/library/create` and asserts a 2xx response + that `criterion_prompt_id('project.foo')` resolves to the written prompt; OR document a rationale for why full E2E coverage is deferred (e.g., to a named child story with a Playwright suite).
findings.finding: The editor authoring flow (AC #5: 'editor authoring form surfaces the resulting criterion id … a net-new author no longer dead-ends at a 400') is a user-facing flow with no E2E check described and no documented rationale for its absence.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria – AC #5 / Scope – editor_assets/src/rebarProvider.jsx

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add or explicitly defer a retirement/cleanup path for project criteria (removing both the rubric file and the overlay routing+activate entry together), and document ownership of that operation.
findings.finding: There is no described retirement/cleanup path for a project criterion: neither how to delete the rubric file (`plan-review-project-<name>.md`) nor how to remove its `plan_review[criterion_id]` routing and `activate` entry from the overlay. Without this, deleted or renamed project criteria accumulate orphaned rubric files and stale overlay entries.
findings.location: 'Scope' section; 'What' section — lifecycle of overlay state

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a sequencing note identifying the thin vertical slice (e.g., implement and test `criterion_prompt_id` + backend resolution first, then wire the editor form's explicit id-field last) so the riskiest integration point is de-risked before the full UI plumbing is built.
findings.finding: The plan touches 5 files across 3 layers (backend logic, data/persistence layer, frontend UI) with no stated thin vertical slice or evidence-gated sequencing; the riskiest piece — the editor form's explicit `criterion-id` field plumbing through the `/library/create` POST body into `author_criterion_overlay` — is not isolated as an early, independently verifiable increment.
findings.location: Scope section; 'The dotted criterion id flows through explicitly' section

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Remove `ids.py` as a candidate home for `criterion_prompt_id`; place it in `model.py` (the existing shared criteria layer), which already hosts all three call-sites' natural dependency.
findings.finding: The plan proposes `ids.py` as an alternative home for `criterion_prompt_id` — a brand-new shared module for a single ~4-line function — when `model.py` already exists as the shared criteria abstraction layer and is the plan's own primary suggestion. A one-function module with no other content is a premature-abstraction smell: it adds an import seam and a new file to navigate with zero benefit over adding the function to `model.py`.
findings.location: Scope section: 'src/rebar/llm/criteria/model.py (or a small shared ids.py) — criterion_prompt_id'
findings.suggested_fix: Place `criterion_prompt_id` directly in `src/rebar/llm/criteria/model.py` alongside `build_descriptor` and `threshold_for`. The three call-sites (`registry.py`, `criterion_preview.py`, `editor.py`) already import from or can import from `rebar.llm.criteria`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Either correct the prior-art analogies to ones that actually use flat-namespace substitution (rather than directory hierarchy), or add a brief note explaining why subdirectory storage (`.rebar/prompts/project/<name>.md` — the direct application of the npm/Python analogies) was ruled out in favour of flat-namespace dot→dash substitution.
findings.finding: The three cited prior-art analogies (Semgrep, npm, Python) all decouple logical id from physical path via directory hierarchy or pure metadata — none uses dot→dash substitution in a flat file namespace. The plan's chosen approach (flat-namespace substitution) is the opposite of what the cited prior art actually does, and the more direct application of those analogies — subdirectory storage (e.g. `.rebar/prompts/project/<name>.md`) — is never considered or ruled out.
findings.location: ## What — decouple the logical id from the physical filename (the Semgrep/npm/Python pattern)
findings.suggested_fix: Add a sentence to the 'What' section: 'A subdirectory layout (`.rebar/prompts/project/<name>.md`) would be the most direct application of the npm/Python analogies, but `get_prompt` resolves a flat `.rebar/prompts/<id>.md` with no subdirectory support, and adding subdirectory resolution would be a larger change than a single canonical mapping function. The dot→dash flat-namespace approach reuses the existing resolver unchanged.' Then either keep or replace the Semgrep/npm/Python examples with flat-namespace analogies (e.g. Java's `com.example.Foo` → `com.example.Foo.class` flat jar entry, or CSS custom properties).

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add WCAG 2.1 AA done-definitions to the editor form change: specify that the criterion-id field is keyboard-reachable, has a programmatic label (`<label>` or `aria-label`), and meets 4.5:1 contrast — and add these as observable AC sub-bullets under AC5.
findings.finding: The editor authoring form change (`rebarProvider.jsx`) introduces a new interactive UI element (criterion-id field/display) with no WCAG 2.1 AA accessibility specification — no keyboard navigation, screen-reader labeling, or contrast requirements are mentioned.
findings.location: Scope section: 'the editor UI (`editor_assets/src/rebarProvider.jsx`) so the authoring form derives + shows the resulting criterion id'; AC5: 'The editor authoring form surfaces the resulting criterion id'
findings.suggested_fix: Extend AC5 with: 'The criterion-id display/field is keyboard-accessible (focusable via Tab), has a programmatic label meeting WCAG 2.1 SC 1.3.1 and 4.1.2, and its text meets SC 1.4.3 contrast (4.5:1 minimum).'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit back-out plan (e.g., 'revert the fixture rename commit; re-add a one-release dotted-filename fallback in `criterion_prompt_id` that logs a deprecation warning') even if the expectation is it will never be needed, so the decision is a deliberate, documented choice rather than an implicit omission.
findings.finding: The plan performs an irreversible rename (existing `plan-review-project.<name>.md` fixture files rewritten; dotted fallback path removed) but provides no explicit rollback/back-out plan. The justification ('just-shipped, no external users') is sound, but T4 requires a stated reversibility mechanism, not just an acknowledgement that none is needed.
findings.location: Scope section — 'The ef7e/overlay TEST FIXTURES that hand-place a dotted plan-review-project.no_print.md are just rewritten…' and 'no dotted-get_prompt fallback'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a one-line rollback note in the Scope section, e.g.: 'Back-out: revert this commit; the old dotted filenames are restored; no data migration is required because the feature has no persistent external state.' This satisfies T4 without adding any code complexity.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify how the two-phase write (`create_prompt` then overlay update) handles partial failure: either make it a single atomic operation, add a cleanup/rollback of the prompt file if the overlay write fails, or document that `create_prompt` is idempotent and a retry is always safe so users can recover by re-submitting.
findings.finding: The `author_criterion_overlay` write path is a two-phase operation: first `create_prompt(prompt_id, rubric)` writes the rubric file, then a separate 'atomic overlay routing+activate write' updates the overlay. If the overlay write fails after `create_prompt` succeeds, an orphaned rubric file exists with no corresponding overlay entry — the criterion is unresolvable and there is no cleanup path specified.
findings.location: What section — 'editor authoring: `criterion_preview.author_criterion_overlay` writes the rubric via `create_prompt(...)`, then the atomic overlay routing+activate write'
findings.suggested_fix: In `author_criterion_overlay`, wrap the two writes so that if the overlay write raises, the just-written prompt file is deleted (or renamed to a `.partial` suffix) before re-raising. Alternatively, write to a temp file and rename atomically before the overlay write, so a failed overlay write leaves no orphan.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the cross-step atomicity strategy for `author_criterion_overlay`: either write the rubric file LAST (so a partial write has no visible routing entry) or document a cleanup/rollback on overlay-write failure to remove the already-written rubric file.
findings.finding: The authoring path performs TWO separate writes — first `create_prompt(prompt_id, rubric)` (a filesystem write of the rubric file) and then the 'atomic overlay routing+activate write' — but there is no atomicity guarantee ACROSS the two steps. A crash or error between them leaves either an orphaned rubric file with no routing entry, or (if the overlay is written first) a routing entry pointing to a rubric that doesn't exist yet, silently breaking criterion resolution for any subsequent gate run.
findings.location: 'What' section / `author_criterion_overlay` description; AC: 'A net-new project.<name> criterion can be authored end-to-end'
findings.suggested_fix: Write order: write the overlay routing+activate entry first (it points to a rubric that doesn't exist yet — `load_criteria` will fail loudly if invoked), then write the rubric file; on any failure after the overlay write, roll back the overlay entry. Alternatively, write the rubric file last so an incomplete operation leaves a routing entry with no rubric (detectable) rather than a rubric with no routing (silent).

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the concurrency/idempotency behavior for `create_prompt` when the target file already exists (error vs. overwrite) and document whether `author_criterion_overlay` is safe to call twice with the same id (idempotent upsert vs. error-on-duplicate).
findings.finding: The plan does not address concurrent `/library/create` requests for the same criterion id. Two simultaneous POSTs would both call `create_prompt` (potentially interleaving writes to the same rubric file) and both write the overlay entry with no CAS or lock, risking a torn rubric file or a double-activation entry.
findings.location: 'What' section — `author_criterion_overlay`; AC: 'create_prompt no longer rejects it'
findings.suggested_fix: Use `O_EXCL`-style exclusive create for the rubric file (error if already exists) so the second concurrent call fails fast and cleanly, matching the 'new criterion' semantic; document that re-authoring an existing criterion is a separate update flow.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the editor UI error state for a failed or partially-completed `/library/create` POST: what message is shown, whether the library list is refreshed, and whether retrying is safe (idempotent or user-warned).
findings.finding: The plan specifies the happy path for the editor UI fix but does not address what the user sees if the server-side authoring partially succeeds — e.g., `create_prompt` succeeds but the overlay write fails, or the POST itself times out after the rubric file is written. The error recovery UX (what message is shown, whether the partial state is visible in the library list, whether retrying is safe) is unspecified.
findings.location: AC: 'A net-new project.<name> criterion can be authored end-to-end via the editor (create_prompt no longer rejects it)'; editor_assets/src/rebarProvider.jsx

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
